prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code for violations of the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these int...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 84,
      "line_end": 744,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Class handles pipeline execution orchestration, metric registration, and dead-letter queue lifecycle management.",
      "reasoning": "God Class smell -> SRP violation because it encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: 1) Pipeline lifecycle management (initializing and closing), 2) Metric registration and complex flow calculation logic (e.g., initializeFlowMetrics, initializePluginFlowMetrics), 3) Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) writer configuration and statistics collection, and 4) Secret store integration. Changes to the monitoring system or DLQ implementation would force changes to this execution engine class."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/Event.java",
      "entity_name": "Event",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 48,
      "line_end": 444,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Event class mixes domain data representation with persistence (JSON/CBOR serialization) and complex timestamp parsing logic.",
      "reasoning": "Large Class smell -> SRP violation because the class is responsible for both representing the Logstash Event data model and the mechanisms for its persistence/transport. It directly contains Jackson ObjectMapper logic (toJson, fromJson, serialize) and complex business logic for parsing various timestamp formats (initTimestamp, parseTimestamp). Persistence and parsing should be delegated to specialized codec or factory classes."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/ackedqueue/Queue.java",
      "entity_name": "Queue",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 57,
      "line_end": 686,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Queue class manages high-level queue semantics alongside low-level disk I/O coordination and serialization.",
      "reasoning": "Divergent Change smell -> SRP violation because the class handles: 1) The logical state of the queue (tail pages, unread counts), 2) Disk space management (ensureDiskAvailable), 3) Concurrency control (ReentrantLock/Conditions), and 4) Serialization/Deserialization via reflection (deserializeMethod). A change in how events are serialized or how disk availability is checked shouldn't necessitate a change in the core persistent queue logic."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/common/io/DeadLetterQueueWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "DeadLetterQueueWriter",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 82,
      "line_end": 643,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Writer class manages both the writing of data and the enforcement of complex retention/cleanup policies.",
      "reasoning": "God Class smell -> SRP violation because it couples the responsibility of data persistence (RecordIOWriter) with the policy-driven management of the filesystem. Specifically, it handles: 1) Sequential writing of events, 2) Retention policy enforcement (Age/Size), and 3) Monitoring the filesystem for external cleanup via WatchService (watchForDeletedNotification). The cleanup and monitoring logic should be separated from the writer."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/secret/store/backend/JavaKeyStore.java",
      "entity_name": "JavaKeyStore",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 64,
      "line_end": 359,
      "principle": "SRP",
      "severity": "low",
      "description": "Class handles secret persistence alongside low-level OS-specific file permission management.",
      "reasoning": "Class mixes high-level security store operations (persistSecret, retrieveSecret) with low-level platform-specific file system concerns (POSIX file permissions, Windows-specific locking workarounds, and obfuscation byte-level manipulation). These low-level file attributes management should be abstracted into a separate FileSystem utility."
    }
  ]
}
```
